app.title: Device Management
form.name: Name *
form.functionPlaceholder: What is this device used for?
detail.confirmDeleteMqtt: Really delete this device? WARNING: The device will also be removed from Home Assistant. Automations using this device may be affected!
bulk.deleteConfirmMqtt: Delete {count} devices? WARNING: They will also be removed from Home Assistant. Automations may be affected!
panel.warningBody: You've opened Device Manager via the HA Panel path. Writes (save, delete, MQTT) don't work here. Open the app via Settings → Add-ons → Geräteverwaltung → Open Web UI.
settings.title: Settings
settings.languageDesc: Change the display language of the app
settings.darkModeDesc: Switch between light and dark theme
settings.syncDesc: Synchronize pending changes with the server
settings.haImportDesc: Import all devices from Home Assistant into the inventory. Already imported devices will be skipped.
settings.haImportConfirm: Import all devices from Home Assistant? Existing devices will be skipped.
settings.haImportFailed: Import failed. Is Home Assistant reachable?
settings.haImportNoDevices: No HA devices found — check HA connection and token
settings.haImportAllDuplicates: All {total} HA devices are already imported — nothing new.
settings.haImportAllNonPhysical: All {total} HA entries were non-physical (automations, helpers) and were skipped.
settings.recategorizeDesc: Re-applies the current category classifier to all imported HA devices. Useful after upgrading to v2.3.2 — fixes miscategorised devices from earlier versions (e.g. smoke alarms shown as Smart TV).
settings.autoCategorizeDesc: When enabled, the import tries to auto-assign a category to new devices. When disabled, all new devices get the category "Other" and you assign them manually.
settings.autoCategorizeCriteria: What criteria are used for categorization?
settings.autoCategorizeCriteria1: HA device_class on entities (e.g. smoke → Sensor, outlet → Socket, tv → Smart TV)
settings.autoCategorizeCriteria2: Entity domain (light, climate, lock, camera, vacuum, …)
settings.autoCategorizeCriteria4: Name (word boundaries only, e.g. \btv\b — prevents "Smoke alarm TV" from being classified as Smart TV)
categories.desc: Manage device categories. Built-in categories cannot be renamed or deleted; custom categories (below) can be changed freely.
settings.mqttDesc: Publish inventory devices as HA entities (warranty sensors, location, etc.)
settings.mqttEnableConfirm: Publish devices to Home Assistant? This will create HA entities for each inventory device (warranty sensors, location, etc.).
settings.mqttTestConnectOnly: Partial OK: {broker} — connection OK, but publish failed (ACL?)
settings.mqttHintAuth: Login rejected. Check MQTT user and password in add-on options. With Mosquitto the user must exist (HA → Settings → People → Users, or Mosquitto ACL).
settings.mqttHintRefused: Broker refused the connection. Is Mosquitto running? Correct port (1883 plain, 8883 TLS)?
settings.mqttHintTimeout: Broker unreachable. Check hostname/IP and firewall. For non core-mosquitto brokers the HA network must reach the broker.
settings.mqttHintDns: Hostname could not be resolved. Is 'mqtt_host' in the add-on options correct?
settings.mqttHintAcl: Connection OK but broker blocks publishing to 'homeassistant/#'. The MQTT ACL must grant publish on the discovery prefix.
settings.mqttHintGeneric: See add-on log (Settings → Log Viewer → filter 'mqtt') and Mosquitto broker log for details.
settings.snapshotsDesc: Before destructive actions (recategorize, bulk edit/delete, replace-import, category delete) a database snapshot is taken automatically. Restore an earlier state here. Keeps the last 10 snapshots, max. 30 days.
snapshots.empty: No snapshots yet. They're created automatically before risky actions.
snapshots.restoreSuccess: Restored. Previous state was backed up for safety: {name}
settings.trashDesc: Deleted devices remain visible here and can be restored individually or in bulk. Permanently deleting removes a device and all its photos for good.
settings.exportDesc: Download all devices as JSON file
settings.clearDataDesc: Remove all locally stored device data
settings.clearConfirm: Delete all local data? This cannot be undone.
settings.clearConfirmMqtt: Delete all local data? WARNING: Devices are still published in Home Assistant. Disable 'Publish devices to HA' first if you want to remove them from HA too.
settings.diagnosticDesc: Having trouble with the add-on? A diagnostic report helps to pinpoint issues quickly.
settings.diagnosticAnonymizeDesc: Replaces names in logs with "Device-001" etc.
settings.diagnosticGithubHint: Please describe the problem above. The diagnostic report below helps with debugging.
settings.diagnosticClipboardDesc: For forum posts, email or chat. No account needed.
settings.diagnosticNote: The report is only transmitted or copied after you click. You can review it in the preview beforehand.
camera.title: Camera
camera.fileInputHint: Choose an existing image from your gallery
camera.httpsRequired: Camera requires a secure connection (HTTPS). Please access via Nabu Casa or HTTPS.
camera.notSupported: Camera is not supported by this browser.
scanner.title: Scan Barcode / QR Code
scanner.httpsRequired: Barcode scanner requires a secure connection (HTTPS). Please access via Nabu Casa or HTTPS.
types.speaker: Speaker
license.title: License
license.feature.multilingual: All languages (DE, EN, ES, FR, RU)
license.deviceLimitReached: Device limit reached (Free: {limit}). Upgrade to Pro for unlimited devices.
license.languageProOnly: Additional languages available with Pro license
license.buyProHint: One-time purchase · license key delivered instantly by email
detail.setupInHADesc: Opens the HA integration page for this device
